name: Mesa de Oña Loop
name_original: Portillo del Mercado — circular desde Salas de Bureba
description: The Mesa de Oña Loop is a difficult 24.4-mile route with 3455 feet of gain, though it's not recommended for mountain biking.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Salas De Bureba traverse a diverse landscape characterized by gentle hills, cultivated fields, and ancient pathways within the province of Burgos. The region, situated at the entrance to the Valle de las Caderechas and part of La Bureba, features varied geological formations and picturesque scenery. These natural features provide a rich tapestry of terrain suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Salas De Bureba The most popular mountain bike trail is Mesa de Oña Loop, a 24.4 miles (39.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers varied terrain and significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Oña Historic Town Center – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Salas de Bureba, a moderate 22.4 miles (36.1 km) path. This route guides riders through historic areas and offers scenic views of the surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Altotero Pass – Poza de la Sal loop from Poza de la Sal, a 24.6 miles (39.7 km) trail leading through Altotero Pass and past the historic village of Poza de la Sal, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Salas De Bureba is defined by gentle hills, cultivated fields, and ancient pathways, offering routes that connect charming villages and historic sites. The network provides options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging trails with technical sections. The routes in Salas De Bureba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Salas De Bureba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Salas De Bureba?
A: There are nearly 50 mountain bike trails around Salas De Bureba, offering a diverse range of experiences for riders. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Salas De Bureba?
A: The terrain around Salas De Bureba is quite varied, characterized by gentle hills, cultivated fields, and ancient pathways. You'll find routes that traverse the picturesque Valle de las Caderechas, known for its cherry and apple trees, as well as areas with more rugged geological formations in the wider La Bureba region. This diversity provides options from leisurely rides to more challenging trails with technical sections.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Salas De Bureba?
A: Yes, Salas De Bureba offers several routes suitable for beginners. While the majority are moderate, there are 5 easy trails available. These typically follow gentle slopes and ancient paths, providing a pleasant introduction to mountain biking in the region.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 9 difficult mountain bike trails. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1110058848/mesa-de-ona-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesa de Oña Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 39.2 km with significant elevation changes, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5342011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altotero Pass – Poza de la Sal loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, are designed to test your skills and endurance.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking in the area?
A: Many trails in Salas De Bureba pass by interesting historical and natural sites. You can explore the unique industrial architecture of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats and Old Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4968842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MonasterioDeSanSalvador&lt;/a&gt;. For scenic views, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5140087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Escalera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoints/Susenes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, a notable feature in the region is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4988248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line. This natural path offers an excellent surface for cycling and connects to the city of Burgos, providing a smooth and scenic option for riders.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Salas De Bureba?
A: Salas De Bureba benefits from a special microclimate, making it conducive to outdoor activities throughout different seasons. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with blooming cherry trees in the Valle de las Caderechas in spring and comfortable temperatures. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winters are generally mild enough for riding.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Salas De Bureba?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Salas De Bureba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Salas de Bureba&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altotero Pass – Poza de la Sal Saltworks loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular options for exploring the region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Salas De Bureba?
A: The mountain bike trails in Salas De Bureba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 17 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. The picturesque scenery, especially in the Valle de las Caderechas, is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For those looking for a longer ride, the region offers options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stream in Lences de Bureba – Castillo de Sotopalacios loop from Cantabrana&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 84 km. This route provides an extensive journey through the varied landscapes of the Bureba region, connecting different points of interest.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Beyond the cultivated fields and gentle hills, you can encounter unique geological formations, particularly in the wider La Bureba region, which features saline marls and ancient volcanic materials. Some routes may also lead you past natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5295093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Rock&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4977826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Penedo de los Portugueses Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, adding to the scenic diversity.
tips: A very fun path through the middle of the forest that goes up from Penches to the Portillo del Mercado. The singletrack has technical areas and, above all, some slopes greater than 20% that in some cases have to be done on foot carrying the bicycle.
After a long ascent, about 400 meters of difference in level, and crossing the entire forest, you finally reach this gate, which leads to a large open esplanade.
In the middle of the medieval town

faqs.question: Is the Mesa de Oña Loop suitable for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: No, the Mesa de Oña Loop (PR-BU 65) is explicitly not recommended for mountain biking. While the broader Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park offers cycling routes, this specific trail is designated for hiking. Its terrain includes narrow, intermittent paths, a strong initial ascent, and a steep descent through a beech forest, making it unsuitable for bikes.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Mesa de Oña Loop for hikers?
faqs.answer: For hikers, this route is generally rated as having a medium to low difficulty, or 'moderate'. The main challenges are a long initial ascent and a steep descent. It covers approximately 14.5 kilometers (9 miles) with a total climb and descent of around 670 meters.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, especially when the beech forest is in full color. Be aware that poor visibility due to fog or low clouds can make navigation difficult, particularly finding the descent through the Portillo del Mercado.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Mesa de Oña Loop or to access the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations before your visit, especially for any updated rules regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mesa de Oña Loop?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts from the town of Oña. You can usually find parking within or near the town, which serves as the trailhead for this loop.

faqs.question: Is the Mesa de Oña Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this trail are not detailed, it's common for trails in natural parks in Spain to allow dogs on a leash. Be aware that there may be loose livestock in the area, so keeping your dog under control is essential for their safety and that of the animals.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular views from areas like the Portillo del Mercado. You'll also pass by historical elements such as the Monastery of San Salvador and traverse a vibrant beech forest. The Portillo del Mercado Trail is a significant section of the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Mesa de Oña Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, starting with a strong initial ascent along a path, leading to a wide track. It includes zig-zagging through a pine forest, well-marked paths, and intermittent sections. The descent features a narrow path through a cool beech forest. Appropriate footwear is highly recommended due to the varied surfaces and steep sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with and follows sections of several other paths. Notable intersecting trails include Por la Mesa de Oña, Raices de Castilla, Penches, Caminos de Oña, and the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo.

faqs.question: What should I bring or wear for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, steep sections, and potential for poor visibility, you should bring appropriate hiking footwear with good grip. It's also advisable to carry water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. Be prepared for potential encounters with loose livestock.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, hikers should be aware of a long initial ascent and a steep descent. Poor visibility due to fog or low clouds can make navigation challenging, especially when finding the descent through the Portillo del Mercado; in such conditions, it's advised to return to Oña. Also, be mindful of loose livestock in the area.
